channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: De 1957: Gabriela Mistral, poeta Nobel de Literatura, ha muerto
item.description: La escritora chilena recibió el galardón en 1945 por tres sonetos publicados inicialmente en Chile en 1922.
item.dc:creator: Special to The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gabriela Mistral, sentada, en 1945, mostrando su Premio Nobel al escritor sueco Hjalmar Gullberg, a la derecha.

item.title: ¿Hasta qué punto la IA hará perder empleos?
item.description: Existe el temor a que un gran número de trabajadores del conocimiento sean desalojados pronto de sus cubículos y sustituidos por máquinas.
item.dc:creator: Michael Steinberger

item.title: El constante acoso sexual lleva a estas tortugas hembra a lanzarse de acantilados
item.description: En una remota isla de Macedonia del Norte, las tortugas mediterráneas macho superan en número a las hembras en una proporción de 19 a 1, un desequilibrio que está llevando a la población a la extinción.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Preston

item.title: Pizza Hut lanza los locales ‘classic’ para clientes nostálgicos
item.description: En un lanzamiento poco publicitado, Pizza Hut Classics traslada a sus más fervientes fans a una época más sencilla y acogedora.
item.dc:creator: Steven Kurutz

media:content.media:credit: Noah Kalina para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Pizza Hut Classics replica las pizzerías de la décadas de 1980 y 1990, con todo y manteles.

item.title: ¿Buscas un ejercicio aeróbico diferente? Baila
item.description: Moverse al ritmo de la música puede ser “ejercicio disfrazado”.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Friedman and Theodore Tae

media:content.media:credit: Theodore Tae/The New York Times

item.title: A Year After His Arrest, Mahmoud Khalil Lives in Limbo and in Fear
item.description: President Trump made Mr. Khalil the face of his crackdown on pro-Palestinian protests. Mr. Khalil is now living with uncertainty as the courts consider his deportation.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich

media:content.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mahmoud Khalil, who was arrested last year in his Columbia University-owned apartment building, said not knowing what is going to happen to him “is like torture.”

item.title: ‘As I Entered Her Dimly Lit Living Room, I Stopped in My Tracks’
item.description: A good deed leads to a stranger’s apartment, a dim view of February and more reader tales of New York City in this week’s Metropolitan Diary.

item.title: Lucky, Heroic, Profane: The Story of N.Y.P.D. Shield No. 13558
item.description: A history of New York, as told through a single badge and the seven policemen who wore it.
item.dc:creator: Michael Wilson

media:content.media:credit: Amir Hamja for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Shield No. 13558 today. Does anything about the “3” look strange?

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for March 8, 2026
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: What Will Iran’s Future Hold?
item.description: Six writers weigh in on what’s next for the country and its citizens as the war continues.
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: The Badlands Hold Me as I Grieve
item.description: I imagine the birds I see are the family members I’ve lost.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Norris Webb

item.title: Live Updates: U.S.-Israeli Strikes Grow as Fuel Depots Burn in Tehran
item.description: The Israeli military struck several Iranian fuel sites, rocking Tehran and the neighboring city of Karaj with explosions. Iran’s de facto leader vowed to seek vengeance for the killing of the nation’s supreme leader.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times

item.title: Quote of the Day: Retiring Move? Baby Boomers Pick New York.
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Sunday, March 8, 2026.

item.title: In Russia’s Former Capital, Restoring the Past to Survive the Present
item.description: With political activism off-limits, residents of St. Petersburg are finding purpose and community in the “politics of small deeds,” repairing and cleaning architectural treasures.
item.dc:creator: Ivan Nechepurenko and Mary Gelman

media:content.media:credit: Mary Gelman for The New York Times

item.title: He Wrote Judy Blume’s Life Story. She Won’t Talk About It.
item.description: Mark Oppenheimer had many conversations with his subject for his new book. Then the relationship took a turn.
item.dc:creator: Elisabeth Egan
item.category: Are You There God? It's Me, Margaret (Book)

media:content.media:credit: Jillian Freyer for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: “It was like doing another college degree, but you’re majoring in Judy Blume,” Mark Oppenheimer (here in his Connecticut home) says of his extensive research.

item.title: Corrections: March 8, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Sunday, March 8, 2026.

item.title: Gen Z-Backed Rapper Is on Course to Lead Nepal With Landslide Win
item.description: Balendra Shah, the onetime rap artist and former mayor of the country’s capital, is on course to become the country’s next prime minister.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Beech, Sajal Pradhan, Binod Ghimire and Hannah Reyes Morales

media:content.media:credit: Hannah Reyes Morales for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Balendra Shah, center, during his campaign to become the next prime minister of Nepal last Saturday. To his right is Rabi Lamichhane, the Rastriya Swatantra Party president.

item.title: Judge Voids Mass Layoffs at Voice of America
item.description: The ruling, which said that Kari Lake’s appointment to oversee V.O.A.’s parent agency was invalid, was a major rejection of President Trump’s attempts to dismantle the government-funded news group.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim
item.category: United States Agency for Global Media

media:content.media:credit: Anna Rose Layden for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Kari Lake, a Trump ally, has led efforts to dismantle Voice of America.

item.title: Iran Could Retrieve Uranium at Site U.S. Bombed Last Year, Officials Say
item.description: The fate of the highly enriched uranium and the options for securing it have become critical issues for the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes, Tyler Pager, Christiaan Triebert, Eric Schmitt and Ronen Bergman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Israel Strikes Oil Facilities in Iran
item.description: Huge flames erupted into the sky over Tehran and a nearby city on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi and Devon Lum
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Atta Kenare/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Explosions erupted after the Israeli military struck several fuel storage complexes in Tehran on Saturday.

item.title: Smoking Jars of Metal and Fuses Thrown at Protest Near Mayor’s House
item.description: Six people were arrested after anti-Islamic protesters led by the right-wing activist Jake Lang clashed with counterprotesters near Gracie Mansion.
item.dc:creator: Emma Goldberg

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Jake Lang, center, a right-wing activist, drew a small crowd of supporters and a larger crowd of counterprotesters at a demonstration outside Gracie Mansion on Saturday.

item.title: Iran’s De Facto Leader Says Iran Will Not Surrender or Stop Its Attacks
item.description: Ali Larijani, the head of Iran’s Supreme National Security Council and a close confidant of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, said Iran was determined to avenge the killing of the leader.
item.dc:creator: Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ali Larijani, secretary of the Supreme National Security Council of Iran, during a news conference in 2024.

item.title: Americans Struggle to Evacuate the Middle East a Week After Strikes
item.description: One week into the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran, thousands of Americans remain stranded. Despite official promises of help, many say they’ve been left to navigate closed airports, canceled flights and uncertainty on their own.
item.dc:creator: Bethlehem Feleke and McKinnon de Kuyper

item.title: Join Together
item.description: Kelly Richardson’s puzzle is a celebration for solvers.
item.dc:creator: Caitlin Lovinger

media:content.media:credit: Fox Photos/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Look up and leap into this graceful solve.

item.title: Trump Assembles a New Coalition to ‘Eradicate’ Cartels
item.description: At a gathering in Florida, the president asked the leaders of a dozen Latin American and Caribbean nations to help the U.S. military crush armed trafficking groups.
item.dc:creator: Annie Correal and Shawn McCreesh

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump with Latin American and Caribbean leaders at the summit on Saturday. U.S. officials said a new coalition would aggressively target cartels and organized crime across the region.

item.title: U.S.-Israeli Bombing Campaign Targets Tehran as Iran Retaliates
item.description: Airstrikes continued to pound Tehran on Saturday as President Trump posted on social media that Iran “will be hit very hard.” Iran has countered with retaliatory strikes, targeting United States and Israeli interests in several Persian Gulf countries.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A huge plume of smoke has rose in the sky after US-Israel airstrikes in the west of Tehran, on Tuesday.

item.title: Tatjana Wood, Award-Winning Comic Book Colorist, Dies at 99
item.description: She was part of the acclaimed creative teams on comic book series for DC Comics, including Swamp Thing, which she called “Shvampy” in her German accent.
item.dc:creator: George Gene Gustines

item.title: In War’s First Week, a Punishing Military Campaign With No Coherent Endgame
item.description: The U.S. and Israel have pounded Iran’s leadership and undercut its defense capabilities, but President Trump has offered wildly different explanations for what he hopes to achieve.
item.dc:creator: Mark Mazzetti, Tyler Pager, Ronen Bergman, Farnaz Fassihi, Eric Schmitt, Erika Solomon and Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A huge plume of smoke has rose in the sky after US-Israel airstrikes in the west of Tehran, on Tuesday.

item.title: Runners at Los Angeles Marathon Can Get a Finisher Medal After Only 18 Miles
item.description: The decision to award medals to runners who do not complete the entire 26.2-mile race comes as temperatures are set to reach into the 80s on Sunday. Some marathoners disagreed with the change.
item.dc:creator: Claire Fahy

media:content.media:credit: Patrick T. Fallon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Organizers of the marathon in Los Angeles said there would be “no shame in making a smart decision” for runners who felt the need to exit the race earlier.

item.title: When DOGE Unleashed ChatGPT on the Humanities
item.description: Documents show how A.I. was used to cancel most previously approved grants by the National Endowment for the Humanities as the agency embraced President Trump’s agenda.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Schuessler

media:content.media:credit: Jason Andrew for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The headquarters of the National Endowment for the Humanities in Washington. The agency was created in 1965 to promote a wide appreciation for the humanities and “mutual respect for the diverse beliefs and values of all persons and groups.”

item.title: Thaddeus Mosley, Sculptor Who Found Fame in His Last Decade, Dies at 99
item.description: A self-taught artist, he turned reclaimed wood into striking abstract works influenced by Brancusi, Noguchi and African art.
item.dc:creator: Will Heinrich

media:content.media:credit: Ross Mantle for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Thaddeus Mosley at his studio in Pittsburgh in 2023. Of the racial and economic barriers he faced, he said: “I knew that I wasn’t going to get the same recognition, have the same opportunity. But I tried, because it’s something I wanted to do.”

item.title: Deadly Clashes in Lebanon as Israel Renews Search for Missing Airman
item.description: Dozens were killed in the Bekaa Valley overnight, the Lebanese health authorities said, amid airstrikes and intense violence in the town of Nabi Sheet.
item.dc:creator: Abdi Latif Dahir and David Guttenfelder
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Residents walking amid the aftermath of deadly strikes in Nabi Sheet, a town in Lebanon’s eastern Bekaa Valley.

item.title: Two Toronto-Area Synagogues Are Struck by Gunfire
item.description: The episodes, which happened late Friday and early Saturday, came after a third synagogue was damaged by gunfire on Monday, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

media:content.media:credit: Mert Alper Dervis/Anadolu, via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The Beth Avraham Yoseph of Toronto Synagogue was damaged by gunfire late on Friday night, according to the mayor of Vaughan, a suburb of Toronto.

item.title: After Tornadoes Ravage Michigan and Oklahoma, Residents Survey Damage
item.description: At least six people were dead and well over a dozen injured between the two states after a series of tornadoes on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hippensteel, Sam Easter and Kristi Eaton

media:content.media:credit: Jamie Kelter Davis for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Homes destroyed by a tornado in Union City, Mich., on Friday night. Three people were killed in the area as a result of the storm.

item.title: Pakistan Praised Trump. Now It Risks Being Caught Up in His War With Iran
item.description: U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran have set off a crippling energy crisis and sparked deadly protests in one of the world’s most populous Muslim nations.
item.dc:creator: Elian Peltier
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Aamir Qureshi/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Crowds protesting the U.S.-Israeli bombing campaign against Iran, in Islamabad, Pakistan, on Friday.

item.title: How D.H.S. Retreated on Immigration Tactics After Minneapolis
item.description: Agents have conducted more targeted operations, rather than street sweeps, leading to a dip in arrests — at least for now.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Nehamas, Zolan Kanno-Youngs, Hamed Aleaziz, Albert Sun and Julie Bosman

media:content.media:credit: David Guttenfelder/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Immigration officers arresting a man in Minneapolis in January.

item.title: At Noma, Accusations of Past Physical Abuse
item.description: Dozens of former employees say René Redzepi inflicted physical and psychological violence on the staff for years.
item.dc:creator: Julia Moskin

media:content.media:credit: John McConnico for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Noma, in Copenhagen, has become one of the world’s most famous restaurants under its chef, René Redzepi. Former workers have come forward to say that its reputation was built on abusive working conditions.

item.title: What Clothes Feel New Now?
item.description: In Paris, Loewe and Polo bring some much needed color and uplift.
item.dc:creator: Jacob Gallagher

media:content.media:credit: Simbarashe Cha/The New York Times

item.title: Ian Huntley, Whose Murder of Schoolgirls Appalled Britain, Dies After Prison Attack
item.description: The 2002 deaths of Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman, and the manhunt that led to a local school caretaker, shook the U.K.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Kwai

media:content.media:credit: Reuters
media:content.media:description: Ian Huntley in a photo taken during the search for Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman. Mr. Huntley gave interviews to the news media before being charged with killing them.

item.title: Iranian President’s Apology to Neighboring Gulf States Showcases Leadership Rifts
item.description: Masoud Pezeshkian, the Iranian president, apologized for Iranian strikes on Gulf states before backtracking after criticism from other Iranian leaders. Despite his remarks, Iran has continued its attacks.
item.dc:creator: Leily Nikounazar and Aaron Boxerman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Dave Sanders for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Masoud Pezeshkian of Iran at the United Nations General Assembly in New York, in September.

item.title: ICE Detains Reporter Estefany Rodriguez Florez in Nashville
item.description: The detention of Estefany Maria Rodriguez Florez, who had sought asylum and is married to an American citizen, raised fears that she had been targeted for her reporting.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane and Hamed Aleaziz

item.title: Recent U.S. Assessment Found an Attack on Iran Unlikely to Result in Regime Change
item.description: Intelligence officials have been skeptical that a popular uprising could dislodge the Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps, which controls much of Iran’s security apparatus.
item.dc:creator: Julian E. Barnes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: People in Tehran on Friday mourning Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Iran’s supreme leader, who was killed in the U.S.-Israeli strikes. There seems to be wide agreement in the U.S. intelligence community that the theological government in Iran is deeply entrenched.

item.title: Conductor Andris Nelsons Has Become a Cautionary Tale
item.description: Andris Nelsons’s abrupt departure from the Boston Symphony Orchestra shouldn’t be surprising to those who have witnessed his artistic decline.
item.dc:creator: David Allen

item.title: Vodka Sauce Tomato Soup Is Exactly as Luscious as It Sounds
item.description: With serious tomato flavor from roasted canned and cherry tomatoes as well as caramelized tomato paste.
item.dc:creator: Mia Leimkuhler

media:content.media:credit: David Malosh for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Hadas Smirnoff.
media:content.media:description: Ella Quittner’s vodka sauce tomato soup.

item.title: Estas personas podrían levantarte el ánimo
item.description: Son capaces de convertir un trámite burocrático o un día en el juzgado en un buen momento. ¿Cómo y por qué lo hacen?
item.dc:creator: Jancee Dunn

media:content.media:credit: Laila Stevens para The New York Times

item.title: Pie-deas for Pi Day
item.description: March 14 — that is, 3.14 — is a great day for butterscotch banana cream pie, lemon meringue pie and chocolate cream pie with Oreo crust.
item.dc:creator: Vaughn Vreeland

media:content.media:credit: Linda Xiao for The New York Times. Food Stylist: Samantha Seneviratne. Prop Stylist: Paige Hicks.
media:content.media:description: Vaughn Vreeland’s butterscotch banana cream pie.

item.title: What Watching a Furious Hillary Clinton Showed Me
item.description: Her exasperation during her Epstein-related deposition channeled the national mood for some of us.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Grose
item.category: House Committee on Oversight and Government Reform

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Iran War Maps: Tracking the Mideast Conflict
item.description: Maps show where U.S. and Israel have struck Iran, and where Iran has retaliated.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times
